description: Write generic code using Templates and leverage the power of the Standard Template Library (STL) for efficient data handling.

slides.title: One Code, Any Type 🧬
Function Templates
Class Templates
Introduction to STL
slides.content:
# The Standard Template Library 📚

STL is a collection of pre-written, optimized templates for common data structures and algorithms.

## Components
1.  **Containers:** Store data (Vectors, Lists, Maps).
2.  **Algorithms:** Process data (Sort, Search, Reverse).
3.  **Iterators:** Traverse data (Like pointers for containers).

slides.title: Vectors
slides.content:
# Dynamic Arrays: Vector 📈

Unlike arrays, `std::vector` can grow and shrink dynamically.

```cpp
#include <vector>

vector<int> numbers;
numbers.push_back(10); // Adds 10
numbers.push_back(20); // Adds 20

cout << numbers[0];    // Access like array
cout << numbers.size(); // Get size (2)
```

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Templates & STL 🎓

Don't reinvent the wheel. Use the tools provided!

## Key Takeaways
- **Templates:** Generic functions (`template <typename T>`) and classes.
- **STL:** Standard Template Library.
- **Vector:** Resizable array. Most used container.
- **Algorithm:** `sort`, `reverse`, `binary_search`.
- **Iterator:** Pointer-like way to traverse containers.

Using STL makes you a competitive programmer. Efficient and fast!
